title: Conditional HTTP Fetching, Redirects, Compression, and Publisher Budgets · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for conditional http fetching, redirects, compression, and publisher budgets and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for publisher-friendly conditional fetch client. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-…
objectives: Store and send ETag and Last-Modified validators correctly, interpret 304 without parsing a body, and update validators only from admitted responses.
Classify 200, 304, redirect, temporary, permanent, auth, missing, rate-limit, and unexpected responses before XML admission.
Reapply scheme, authority, address, hop, credential, and loop policy at each redirect and record permanent moves separately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feed Go Publisher Rate Policy
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 9029 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove conditional HTTP, redirect and compression admission, cleanup, and publisher budgets; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply per-origin concurrency, minimum intervals, Retry-After, exponential backoff, jitter, identification, and operator override.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feed Go Publisher Rate Policy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for apply per-origin concurrency, minimum intervals, retry-after, exponential backoff, jitter, identification, and operator override. For Feed aggregation and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feed Go Publisher Rate Policy, keep Feed Go Publisher Rate Policy connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
